overview: Cloud computing delivers IT services on demand rather than through owned, on-premises hardware, characterized by NIST's five essential characteristics and delivered via three service models.
definitions: IaaS: Infrastructure as a Service — rent virtual machines/network/storage and manage the OS and applications yourself (e.g. AWS EC2).
PaaS: Platform as a Service — a development platform is provided; you don't manage the underlying OS (e.g. Google App Engine).
SaaS: Software as a Service — a finished, ready-to-use application (e.g. Office 365).
vSwitch: a virtual switch inside a hypervisor host connecting VMs to each other and to the physical NICs.
core_concepts: NIST's five characteristics: On-demand self-service, Broad network access, Resource pooling, Rapid elasticity, Measured service.
Virtualization: a hypervisor divides one physical machine's hardware into multiple isolated Virtual Machines (VMs).
best_practices: Choose the service model (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS) that matches how much of the stack the organization actually wants to manage.
interview_questions: Explain the difference between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS with an example of each.
List and explain NIST's five essential characteristics of cloud computing.
What is a vSwitch and where does it sit in a virtualized host?
exam_traps: Mixing up which service model manages which layer — remember: IaaS = infrastructure only, PaaS = platform (no OS management), SaaS = finished app.
enterprise_notes: Enterprises typically connect to cloud providers over WAN links (MetroE/MPLS/Internet VPN), making Cloud and WAN Architecture closely linked topics.
